package userhandler

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"net/http"
)

// Positive scenario for error_code_literals_duplicated_across_handlers.
// Rule intent: Repeated string error codes without a centralized catalog.

// UserHandler duplicates error code literals that are also defined in OrderHandler.
type UserHandler struct{}

func (h *UserHandler) GetUser(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	const ErrCodeNotFound = "NOT_FOUND"
	const ErrCodeValidation = "VALIDATION_ERROR"
	const ErrCodeForbidden = "FORBIDDEN"
	const ErrCodeUnauthorized = "UNAUTHORIZED"

	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)
	json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{
		"code":    ErrCodeNotFound,
		"message": "user not found",
	})
}

func (h *UserHandler) UpdateUser(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	const ErrCodeValidation = "VALIDATION_ERROR"
	const ErrCodeNotFound = "NOT_FOUND"

	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)
	json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{
		"code":    ErrCodeValidation,
		"message": "invalid email format",
	})
}

func (h *UserHandler) DeleteUser(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	const ErrCodeForbidden = "FORBIDDEN"
	const ErrCodeNotFound = "NOT_FOUND"

	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusForbidden)
	json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{
		"code":    ErrCodeForbidden,
		"message": "cannot delete admin user",
	})
}
